channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump fires heads of TSA, Coast Guard and guts key aviation safety advisory committee
item.description: President Donald Trump has moved quickly to remake the Department of Homeland Security by firing the heads of the Transportation Security Administration and Coast Guard before their terms are up while eliminating all the members of a key aviation security advisory group.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's first full day back in White House includes firings and an infrastructure announcement
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is spending his first full day back in the White House meeting with congressional leaders, announcing an investment in artificial intelligence infrastructure and demonstrating one of his favored expressions of power: firing people.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At inaugural prayer service, bishop pleads for Trump to ‘have mercy’ on LGBTQ+ people and migrants
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — At the inaugural prayer service, the Right Rev. Mariann Budde, the Episcopal bishop of Washington, made a direct appeal to President Donald Trump to have mercy on the LGBTQ+ community and undocumented migrant workers.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY and GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump cuts DEI regulations across federal government
item.description: In several executive orders signed Monday, Trump undid several Biden-era initiatives, saying they “end now.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: President Trump urged to pardon 76-year-old grandmother, others in prison for pro-life activism
item.description: Anti-abortion groups are calling on the president to pardon a 76-year-old grandmother and 20 others who were imprisoned and prosecuted for pro-life protests under the Biden Department of Justice.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump highlights partnership investing $500 billion in AI
item.description: President Donald Trump is talking up a joint venture investing up to $500 billion for infrastructure tied to artificial intelligence by a new partnership formed by OpenAI, Oracle and SoftBank.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Democratic AGs sue Trump over birthright citizenship executive order
item.description: Twenty-two Democrat-led states and two cities challenged President Trump’s executive order restricting birthright citizenship, that on Tuesday kicked off the first legal battles between his new Justice Department and Democratic attorneys general.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada, 17 other states sue Trump administration over birthright citizenship order
item.description: Attorneys general from 18 states, including Nevada, sued the Trump administration Tuesday to stop it from seeking to eliminate birthright citizenship, a protection guaranteed in the U.S. Constitution.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court declines to step into former GOP Rep. Steve King's meme fight
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday declined to revive former Rep. Steve King’s (R-Iowa) bid to receive attorneys’ fees in a copyright fight that stemmed from a meme posted by his campaign.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's House GOP allies push birthright citizenship bill after progressive fury at presidential order
item.description: House Republicans are pushing a new bill to limit birthright citizenship to babies born with at least one parent who is a U.S. citizen or permanent resident.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump fires 4 presidential appointees, including his former Iran envoy
item.description: President Donald announced the firing of four high-profile presidential appointees just after midnight Tuesday, including a top envoy to Iran during his first term and retired Gen. Mark Milley, who Trump tapped as Joint Chiefs chairman in 2018.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Trump sets out to erase Biden's legacy with pardons and orders immediately after taking office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump began erasing Joe Biden's legacy immediately after taking office as the nation’s 47th president, pardoning nearly all of his supporters who rioted at the U.S. Capitol on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Court cases could shape
item.description: The Minnesota Supreme Court will hear arguments this week over whether the Republican-only start to session in the House is lawful. Two court decisions late last week have also set the stage for more partisan standoffs.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump orders end of humanitarian parole for migrants of four countries
item.description: Under the Biden administration, migrants from embattled countries could apply for entry due for humanitarian reasons, without having to attempt to cross into the U.S. illegally.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: Trump pardons nearly all Jan. 6 rioters on first day of presidency
item.description: President Trump pardoned nearly all Jan. 6 defendants on Monday, making good on a long-standing campaign promise just hours into his second presidency.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signs executive order to pause TikTok ban, provide immunity to tech firms
item.description: The order follows TikTok going dark for about 14 hours after the Supreme Court upheld a law prohibiting the service from operating in the U.S. unless it breaks away from its parent company in China.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Trump issues pardons for participants in the Jan. 6 riot at the U.S. Capitol
item.description: The pardons fulfilled Trump’s promise to release supporters who tried to help him overturn his election defeat four years ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Senate passes Laken Riley Act in first move after Trump inauguration
item.description: The Senate on Monday passed the Laken Riley Act, making the immigration-related bill the first piece of legislation to make it through the upper chamber in the new Congress and putting it a step closer to being signed into law by President Trump. Senators voted 64-35 on the bill.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada Democrats congratulate, caution Trump as he starts 2nd term
item.description: Southern Nevada’s congressional delegation -- all Democrats -- congratulated and cautioned President Donald Trump as he began his second term Monday.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump reinforces use of his 'Remain in Mexico' policy
item.description: The policy, which was created and implemented during the first Trump administration in 2019, resulted in tens of thousands of migrants waiting for extended periods in Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo and Jasmine Garsd, MPR News

item.title: Trump to issue 10 executive orders cracking down on border
item.description: President-elect Trump is set to sign off on 10 executive orders Monday, moving swiftly to crack down on immigration and drug cartels during his first day in office with a blitzkrieg of immigration actions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump inauguration to kick off 100-day rush
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is set to be sworn in at the Capitol on Monday, solidifying his stunning return to the White House and kicking off what is expected to be a busy 100-day rush focused on a handful of hot-button issues.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Biden pardons Fauci, Jan. 6 committee ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: (The Hill) -- President Joe Biden granted pardons to several prominent public servants Monday who have faced attacks from President-elect Donald Trump in one of his final acts in office. Biden issued pardons for Dr.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Official portraits of Donald Trump, JD Vance are released
item.description: Not waiting until after his inauguration, on Thursday President-elect Trump gave the country a look at what he says are the official portraits of himself and Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with explosion of online porn
item.description: “Kids can get online porn through gaming systems, tablets, phones, computers. Let me just say that content filtering for all those different devices, I can say from personal experience, is difficult to keep up with,” said Justice Amy Coney Barrett, who has seven children.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: End to daylight saving time among bills for 2025 Nevada Legislature
item.description: A Republican lawmaker who represents parts of six rural Nevada counties is sponsoring a bill to take the state off daylight saving time — one of the 200 bills written so far.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada elections leader urges real-time efficiency over changing deadlines
item.description: The influx of mail-in ballots collected on Election Day led to delays in tabulation across Nevada, not the number of ballots received in the days after, Democratic Nevada Secretary of State Cisco Aguilar said ahead of the legislative session.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News
